Requirement : The test plan MUST cover tests for Matter supported features on the client device. [PICS, DUT]
Requirement : All prerequisites (such as dependency certifications and security conformance) MUST be met prior to executing the test plan to ensure expected results. [PICS, DUT]

Requirement : The client device MUST conform to the definitions and conformance levels as described, including the use of terms such as MUST, MUST NOT, SHOULD, and SHOULD NOT as defined by IETF RFC-2119.
Requirement : Timing tolerances for Action-at-End tests MUST be within {clockAllowancePercent} of the set timer time or {clockAllowanceMinMs}, whichever is larger.
Requirement : Action-over-time tests on the client device MUST have an allowed value range within ±15% of the expected change during ongoing operations.

Requirement : Verify that Action-at-End tolerances permit the expected action to occur at the expected time ± the defined tolerance, where the tolerance is {clockAllowancePercent} of the set timer time or {clockAllowanceMinMs}, whichever is larger. [Timing and Tolerance Considerations]

Requirement : Verify that Action-over-time tolerances for tests involving continuous device changes use a tolerance of 15% of the expected change, according to the formula V₁ = V₀ ± (ΔV₁ × 0.15), where ΔV₁ = C × (T₁ − T₀) and C = ΔV ÷ ΔT. [Timing and Tolerance Considerations]

Requirement : Confirm that tests for action-at-end scenarios use the default clock allowance and that tests requiring action-over-time explicitly state the tolerance used. [Timing and Tolerance Considerations]

Requirement : Validate that the EventTrigger field in TestEventTrigger commands is properly encoded as a uint64 according to the schema: 0xYYYY_XXXX_ZZZZ_ZZZZ, with 'YYYY' representing the cluster ID, 'XXXX' the targeting endpoint ID, and 'ZZZZ_ZZZZ' the cluster-specific trigger event value. [TestEventTrigger]

Requirement : Confirm that a value of 0xFFFF for 'XXXX' in the EventTrigger field triggers the event on all supported endpoints, and a value of 0x0000 may trigger the event on the first supported endpoint in some legacy devices. [TestEventTrigger]

Requirement : Verify that the first digit of the Manual Pairing Code printed on the DUT is between 0 and 7. If the digit is between 0 and 3, ensure the code length is 11 digits (VID_PID flag not set). If between 4 and 7, ensure the code length is 21 digits (VID_PID flag set). [MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : If the Manual Pairing Code for the DUT is 11 digits (VID_PID flag not set), verify that digits 2 through 6 are between 00000 and 65535, and digits 7 through 10 are between 0000 and 8191. [MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : If the Manual Pairing Code for the DUT is 21 digits (VID_PID flag set), verify that digits 2 through 6, 11 through 15, and 16 through 20 are between 00000 and 65535, and digits 7 through 10 are between 0000 and 8191. [MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify the last digit (Check Digit) of the Manual Pairing Code on the DUT matches the digit generated by the checksum script/Test Harness using the preceding digits. [MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the Manual Pairing Code is printed on the Commissionee device or included in additional provided materials such as a manual, companion app, or web service. [MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the NFC tag on the DUT uses NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) and NDEF URI Record Type Definition as required, and is one of the types defined by NFC Forum. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the NFC tag’s onboarding payload code version on the DUT is '000'. [MCORE.DD.NFC]

Requirement : Verify that the onboarding payload on the NFC tag contains an 8-bit Discovery Capabilities bitmask, with bits correctly representing support for BLE, IP network, Wi-Fi Public Action Frame, NFC Transport Layer, and reserved bits set to 0 as specified. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the 12-bit discriminator value in the NFC onboarding payload matches the value advertised by the device during commissioning. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the onboarding payload in the NFC tag contains a 27-bit Passcode as an 8-digit decimal value between 00000001 and 99999998. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the passcode in the NFC onboarding payload is not a trivial value (e.g., 00000000, 11111111, 12345678) and is not derived from public information. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the NFC onboarding payload code prefix is 'MT:'. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the Vendor ID and Product ID in the NFC onboarding payload match the values submitted by the manufacturer in the Distributed Compliance Ledger. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that the packed binary data structure in the onboarding payload is padded with '0' bits at the end to the nearest byte boundary. [MCORE.DD.NFC]
Requirement : Verify that any custom payload in the onboarding payload is a 2-bit field with supported values of 0, 1, and 2. [MCORE.DD.NFC]

Requirement : Verify that scanning a concatenated onboarding QR code for multiple DUTs results in each DUT's onboarding payload being delimited by a '*' character, indicating the correct onboarding of all devices present. [MCORE.DD.CONCATENATED_QR_CODE]

Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the onboarding payload in the NFC tag and in the QR code are exactly the same [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.NFC].

Requirement : Verify the DUT is able to scan and parse the concatenated QR code successfully. [MCORE.DD.CTRL_CONCATENATED_QR_CODE_1]

Requirement : Verify the DUT follows the correct order of onboarding the TH devices which must be from first to last starting from left to right as specified in the concatenated QR code. [MCORE.DD.CTRL_CONCATENATED_QR_CODE_1_INORDER]

Requirement : Verify the QR code is scanned successfully and that the TH parses QR code when using Standard Commissioning Flow. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.QR, MCORE.DD.STANDARD_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : Upon power up, verify the DUT Commissionee is advertising to be commissioned to a Matter network when supporting Standard Commissioning Flow. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.QR, MCORE.DD.STANDARD_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : Verify the QR code is scanned successfully and that the TH parses QR code for User-Intent Commissioning Flow. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.QR, MCORE.DD.USER_INTENT_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : Verify DUT Commissionee is NOT in commissioning mode when powered on, if Custom Flow = 2 (Custom Commissioning Flow). [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.QR, MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]
Requirement : Verify the QR code is scanned successfully and that the TH parses QR code for Custom Commissioning Flow. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE, MCORE.DD.QR, MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]

Reference: Section 14.2.1, Announcement by Device Verification [DUT - Commissionee], Purpose
Requirement: Requirement : Verify if the DUT properly announces its uncommissioned state to allow a Matter Commissioner to discover the DUT to be commissioned. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify that the 12-bit 'Discriminator' field in Matter service data is populated in the 'service_specific_info' field of the Wi-Fi PAF Publish Frame sent by the DUT on both 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands. [MCORE.COM.WIFI_2P4GHZ, MCORE.COM.WIFI_5GHZ, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]

Requirement : Verify that the 16-bit 'Vendor ID' field in Matter service data is set to the correct Vendor ID or 0 if elided in the 'service_specific_info' field of the Wi-Fi PAF Publish Frame sent by the DUT. [MCORE.COM.WIFI_2P4GHZ, MCORE.COM.WIFI_5GHZ, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]

Requirement : Verify that the 16-bit 'Product ID' field in Matter service data is set to the correct Product ID or 0 if elided in the 'service_specific_info' field of the Wi-Fi PAF Publish Frame sent by the DUT. [MCORE.COM.WIFI_2P4GHZ, MCORE.COM.WIFI_5GHZ, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]

Requirement : Verify that the 'Extended Data' bit is set to 1 if Extended Data exists, otherwise set to 0, in Bit[0] of the last byte of the 'service_specific_info' field of the Wi-Fi PAF Publish Frame. [MCORE.COM.WIFI_2P4GHZ, MCORE.COM.WIFI_5GHZ, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]

Requirement : Verify that the 'Extended Announcement' bit is set to 0 in Bit[1] of the last byte of the 'service_specific_info' field of the Wi-Fi PAF Publish Frame. [MCORE.COM.WIFI_2P4GHZ, MCORE.COM.WIFI_5GHZ, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]

Requirement : Validate that the D (discriminator), CM (commissioning mode), and other required TXT record keys in the DNS-SD records contain valid key/value pairs as specified for a Commissionable Node, with proper encoding and value representation. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_D, MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_CM]

Requirement : Validate that the optional VP TXT record (Vendor ID and Product ID) in DNS-SD records, if present, complies with encoding rules by expressing both IDs as variable-length decimal ASCII with proper separation and omission of zeroes. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_VP]
Requirement : Validate that the optional DT TXT record (Device Type), if present, contains a variable-length decimal ASCII-encoded device type identifier without leading zeroes. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_DT]
Requirement : Validate that the optional DN TXT record (Device Name), if present, is a valid UTF-8 string with a maximum length of 32 bytes. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_DN]
Requirement : Validate that the optional RI TXT record (Rotating Identifier), if present, contains uppercase hexadecimal octets with a maximum length of 100 characters. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_RI]

Requirement : For the optional PH TXT key (Pairing Hint), if present, validate its encoding as a variable-length decimal ASCII number and ensure required bits are set according to device commissioning methods. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_PH]

Requirement : If the PI TXT record (Pairing Instruction) is present, ensure it contains a valid UTF-8 string up to 128 bytes, and that it is included only when required by the PH bitmap in compliance with dependencies and restrictions on multiple PI-dependent bits. [MCORE.DD.TXT_KEY_PI]

Requirement : Verify that, when the DUT is in a non-commissionable state, DNS-SD does not publish a '_CM' commissioning subtype in the Commissionable Node Discovery service record. [MCORE.DD.EXTENDED_DISCOVERY]

Requirement : Send a DNS-SD browse for '_services._dns-sd._udp' and verify that mandatory commissioning subtypes _L<dddd>, _S<dd>, and _CM match required format and values in Commissionable Node Discovery records. [MCORE.DD.COMMISSIONING_SUBTYPE]

Requirement : Send a DNS-SD browse for '_services._dns-sd._udp' and verify that optional commissioning subtypes _V<ddddd> (Vendor ID) and _T<ddd> (device type) are correctly formatted and published if applicable. [MCORE.DD.COMMISSIONING_SUBTYPE_V, MCORE.DD.COMMISSIONING_SUBTYPE_T]
Requirement : When the DUT is placed into Commissioning mode, verify that it can be discovered over DNS-SD. [MCORE.DD.EXTENDED_DISCOVERY]
Requirement : Send a DNS-SD browse for '_matterc._udp' and verify that the DUT responds with Matter pointer records for the device when in Commissioning mode. [MCORE.DD.EXTENDED_DISCOVERY]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as Commissioner, starts a BLE scan across all three advertising channels with sufficient dwell time, interval, and overall scan duration as specified in the commissioning discovery procedure. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_BLE]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, while scanning as a Commissioner, is able to discover a Commissionee over BLE and provide the onboarding payload as set on the Commissionee (TH). [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_BLE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT acting as Commissioner is able to discover an uncommissioned Matter device using BLE transport and provide onboarding data for validation. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT acting as Commissioner is able to discover an uncommissioned Matter device using Wi-Fi PAF transport and provide onboarding data for validation. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT acting as Commissioner, when connected to the same operational network as the Test Harness, performs service discovery using DNS-SD and provides onboarding data for validation. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissionee initiates the commissioning procedure using User Directed Commissioning and makes 4 retries with each retry delayed by 100ms from the previous transmission. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE] [MCORE.DD.UI] [MCORE.DD.COMM_DISCOVERY]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissionee scans for available commissioners using Commissioner Discovery and finds the Commissioning Discovery DNS-SD service when it is being advertised. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE] [MCORE.DD.COMM_DISCOVERY]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner is able to advertise the Commissioner Discovery service over DNS-SD with service type _matterd._udp. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER] [MCORE.DD.COMM_DISCOVERY]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner, upon receiving an Identification Declaration message, prompts the user for the onboarding payload. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner has regulatory and fabric information available and accurate date, time, and timezone set prior to starting commissioning. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner is able to discover and connect to a commissionee over the appropriate discovery mode, including DNS-based Service Discovery (DNS-SD) over IP network when applicable. [MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_IP]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner can establish encryption keys with the commissionee using Password Authenticated Session Establishment (PASE) on the commissioning channel. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner re-arms the Fail-safe timer on the Commissionee within 60 seconds as required during commissioning. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner configures regulatory information on the Commissionee during the commissioning process. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER] [MCORE.COM.WIRELESS]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner requests an operational Certificate Signing Request (CSR) from the Commissionee using the OperationalCSRRequest command. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner configures operational credentials on the Commissionee if not previously installed. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner configures itself as administrator in the Commissionee Access Control List (ACL) if needed. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner configures the operational network credentials on the Commissionee if supported and required by the Commissionee. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner instructs the Commissionee to connect to the operational network if it is not already connected. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner starts discovery of the Commissionee using Operational Discovery after initial commissioning. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner opens a CASE (Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment) session with the Commissionee over the operational network. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT as Commissioner sends the CommissioningComplete command and that it receives a success response from the Commissionee. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the commissioning channel between the Commissioner (DUT) and Commissionee is terminated after successful commissioning. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that for Non-concurrent Commissioning, the DUT as Commissioner reads and verifies that the Commissionee’s SupportsConcurrentConnection attribute from the General Commissioning cluster is false. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner device is able to re-arm the Fail-safe timer on the Commissionee within 60 seconds of the timer being set by the Commissionee. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner configures regulatory information in the Commissionee device. [MCORE.COM.WIRELESS]
Requirement : Verify that after receiving an OperationalCSRRequest command from the Commissioner, the Commissionee generates a new operational key pair.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner can configure operational credentials on the DUT, including installing a Trusted Root Certificate if needed.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner is able to configure itself as administrator in the ACL on the Test Harness device if needed.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner configures the correct operational network credentials on the Test Harness device if the device both supports and requires it.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner instructs the Commissionee to connect to the operational network and waits for a successful response before proceeding.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner starts discovery of the Test Harness using Operational Discovery procedures.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner is able to open a CASE (Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment) session with the Test Harness device over the operational network.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner can send the CommissioningComplete command and receive a successful response from the Test Harness device.
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner and Commissionee are able to discover each other and connect using the applicable discovery mode for the device.
Requirement : Verify that encryption keys are established on the commissioning channel using Password Authenticated Session Establishment (PASE).
Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner has regulatory and fabric information available and has accurate date, time, and timezone.

Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner can read commissioning information including the SupportsConcurrentConnection attribute from the General Commissioning cluster and confirm it is false for non-concurrent negative scenarios.

Reference: Section 14.3.5 TC-DD-3.7, Test Step 21; Section 14.3.6 TC-DD-3.8, Test Step 11, 21
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the Commissioning channel between the Commissioner and Commissionee is terminated after commissioning procedures.
Requirement : Verify that the device with a custom commissioning flow (Custom Flow field set to value 2) can successfully complete the commissioning procedure as a commissionee.

Requirement : Verify that the Commissioner is triggered to enter the Custom Commissioning Flow upon receiving Onboarding Payload with Custom Flow field set to value 2 and can successfully commission the device requiring custom commissioning data.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as a Commissioner device, receives an onboarding payload from the Test Harness Commissionee with the Custom Flow field set to value 2, and is triggered to enter the Custom Commissioning Flow. [MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : Verify that upon scanning a QR code with the Custom Flow field set to 2, the DUT parses the QR code and confirms that the Commissionee (TH) has not been commissioned to the Matter network at this stage. [MCORE.DD.QR_COMMISSIONING, MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : After DUT has received and parsed a QR code indicating a custom commissioning flow, verify that the user or DUT follows the necessary steps to place the Commissionee (TH) in commissioning mode, and completes commissioning to the network, confirming a successful outcome. [MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]
Requirement : Ensure the DUT is on an operational network and has accurate date, time, timezone, regulatory, and fabric information prior to commissioning attempts. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER]

Requirement : Verify that DUT correctly handles the QR code with a 2-bit Custom Flow field set to 2 and can parse all onboarding payload details according to Table 34 'Packed Binary Data Structure for Onboarding Payload'. [MCORE.DD.QR_COMMISSIONING, MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]

Requirement : Repeat the custom commissioning flow test for each supported discovery transport: BLE, Wi-Fi PAF, and IP Network, using a Commissionee with the Custom Flow field set to 2 for each protocol. [MCORE.DD.CUSTOM_COMM_FLOW]
Requirement : Verify that the Device Under Test (DUT) can successfully scan a QR code provided by the Commissionee device or additional materials. [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE]
Requirement : Verify the DUT parses the QR code after scanning. [MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF, MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_IP]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT does not commission the Commissionee device when an invalid QR code Version String is used. [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT commissions the Test Harness (TH) to the Matter network using Wi-Fi PAF if Discovery Capability bit string is set to Wi-Fi PAF. [MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_PAF]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT commissions the TH to the Matter network using IP Network if Discovery Capability bit string is set to IP Network. [MCORE.DD.DISCOVERY_IP]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT prevents commissioning when provided with an invalid Passcode in a QR code, terminating the process in a DUT-specific manner. [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT does not react successfully to scanning a QR code with an invalid prefix (not 'MT:'). [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT can successfully parse an 11-digit Manual Pairing Code and commission the TH to the Matter network. [MCORE.DD.11_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT can successfully parse a 21-digit Manual Pairing Code and commission the TH to the Matter network. [MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates the commissioning process if provided with an 11-digit Manual Pairing Code with an invalid VERSION value. [MCORE.DD.11_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates the commissioning process if provided with an 11-digit Manual Pairing Code with an invalid VID_PID_PRESENT value. [MCORE.DD.11_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT can accept and process an 11-digit Manual Pairing Code provided in any supported format. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that providing a Manual Pairing Code with an invalid VERSION value results in the DUT terminating the commissioning process as per manufacturer’s instructions. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT terminates the commissioning process upon receiving a Manual Pairing Code with an invalid VID_PID_PRESENT value. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates the commissioning process when the Manual Pairing Code contains a SHORT DISCRIMINATOR value different from the expected value. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Confirm that the DUT terminates the commissioning process when any of the provided Passcode values are invalid. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates the commissioning process when provided with a Manual Pairing Code containing an invalid CHECK_DIGIT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Confirm that the DUT can accept and process a 21-digit Manual Pairing Code provided in any supported format. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that a 21-digit Manual Pairing Code with an invalid VERSION results in the DUT terminating the commissioning process. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT terminates commissioning when a 21-digit Manual Pairing Code is provided with an invalid VID_PID_PRESENT value. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates commissioning if the SHORT DISCRIMINATOR value in a 21-digit Manual Pairing Code is incorrect. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates commissioning for each 21-digit Manual Pairing Code containing invalid Passcode values from a predefined list. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates commissioning when a 21-digit code has invalid VENDOR_ID values from a predefined test list, unless the user is explicitly warned about the related security risks. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]
Requirement : Ensure the DUT terminates commissioning if the PRODUCT_ID in the 21-digit Manual Pairing Code is set to 0x0000. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT terminates commissioning if the CHECK_DIGIT in the 21-digit Manual Pairing Code is not correct as per the Verhoeff algorithm. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER, MCORE.DD.21_MANUAL_PC]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, when acting as a Commissioner device, can generate and process a Manual Pairing Code containing an invalid CHECK_DIGIT while following all guidelines of the Verhoeff algorithm and Encoding Method [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE].

Requirement : Ensure that when the DUT is provided with a Manual Pairing Code that contains an invalid CHECK_DIGIT, the DUT terminates the commissioning process in a DUT-specific manner according to manufacturer’s instructions.

Requirement : Confirm that the DUT, as Commissioner, can successfully commission multiple devices (e.g., TH1 and TH2) onto a Matter network, one after another, by scanning each device's QR code and following the appropriate commissioning procedures [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER] [MCORE.DD.QR_COMMISSIONING].
Requirement : Ensure that after commissioning the first device (TH1), only TH1 is present on the Matter network and TH2 has not yet been commissioned.
Requirement : Verify that, after commissioning the second device (TH2), only TH2 has been commissioned during that step and TH1 remains commissioned from the previous step.
Requirement : Confirm that after commissioning multiple devices, the Commissioner can still interact with each individual device (e.g., read any cluster’s attribute from TH1 and TH2).
Requirement : Verify that the DUT, as Commissionee, can be successfully commissioned onto a Matter network using its onboarding payload QR code.
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT can be unpaired from the Matter network by the Commissioner and that upon unpairing, the DUT is no longer present on the Matter network.
Requirement : Confirm that the DUT, after being unpaired, can be placed back into commissioning mode and discovered again for re-commissioning.
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT, as Commissioner, can unpair a previously commissioned device and then successfully re-commission it onto the Matter network by repeating the onboarding process.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, as Commissioner, can discover and commission a device with multiple endpoints, and that it acknowledges and interacts (such as issuing On commands) with each endpoint implementing an On/Off Light device type [MCORE.DD.SCAN_QR_CODE].

Requirement : Confirm that the DUT, acting as Commissioner, can successfully complete the commissioning procedure using NFC as a temporary transport channel, with commissioning ending successfully [MCORE.DD.SCAN_NFC] [MCORE.DD.NTL].

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, as Commissionee, has an NFC tag payload that can be activated and read, with correct parsing of Discriminator, Passcode, and Discovery Capabilities Bitmask (NFC Transport Layer bit set) [MCORE.DD.NFC] [MCORE.DD.NTL].
Requirement : Ensure that commissioning of the DUT, as Commissionee, through NFC interface completes successfully with no error reported by the Commissioner.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends ArmFailSafeResponse Command to TH1 with field ErrorCode as 'OK'(0) when TH1 sends ArmFailSafe command with the ExpiryLengthSeconds field set to failsafe_max. [ArmFailSafe command sequence]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with the CSRResponse Command when TH1 sends CSRRequest command with a random 32-byte nonce. [CSRRequest/CSRResponse]

Requirement : Verify that AddTrustedRootCertificate Command fails by sending the status code as INVALID_COMMAND when TH1 sends AddTrustedRootCertificate command to DUT to install Root_CA_Malformed. [AddTrustedRootCertificate, malformed input handling]

Requirement : Verify that the TrustedRootCertificate attribute contains only trusted_root_original_size entries after failed AddTrustedRootCertificate with Root_CA_Malformed. [Integrity of TrustedRootCertificate list]

Requirement : Verify that AddTrustedRootCertificate Command succeeds by sending the status code as SUCCESS when TH1 sends AddTrustedRootCertificate command to DUT with RootCACertificate set to Root_CA_Certificate_TH1. [AddTrustedRootCertificate, normal operation]

Requirement : Verify that the TrustedRootCertificate attribute contains trusted_root_original_size + 1 entries after successfully adding a trusted root certificate. [TrustedRootCertificate list expansion]

Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with NOCResponse command with status code InvalidNOC when TH1 sends the AddNOC Command with NOCValue/ICACValue/IPKValue for different Root CA ID (fabric conflict attempt). [AddNOC, fabric conflict handling]
Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with NOCResponse command with status code InvalidAdminSubject when CaseAdminSubject is an invalid NodeID in AddNOC command. [AddNOC, admin subject validation]

Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with NOCResponse command with status code OK when AddNOC Command fields are valid (NOCValue, ICACValue, IPKValue, CaseAdminSubject, AdminVendorId). [AddNOC, normal commissioning]

Requirement : Verify that the list from reading the NOCs attribute with a fabric-filtered read contains one entry with NOC field Node_Operational_Certificate_TH1 and ICAC field Intermediate_Certificate_TH1. [NOCs attribute, fabric filtering]

Requirement : Verify that the RootPublicKey extracted from the FabricDescriptorStruct is exactly 65 bytes and matches Root_Public_Key_TH1, and other metadata matches the Data in the NOC/AddNOC command. [FabricDescriptorStruct integrity]

Requirement : Verify that AddTrustedRootCertificate Command succeeds by sending the status code as SUCCESS when TH1 sends AddTrustedRootCertificate command to DUT with RootCACertificate set to Root_CA_Certificate_TH1_1. [Adding new trusted root certs]
Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with status code MissingCsr if AddNOC is sent without a corresponding CSR request. [AddNOC, CSR dependency enforcement]
Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with status code InvalidPublicKey if NOC/ICAC/IPK are generated using a stale/non-matching CSR. [Public key/CSR consistency]

Requirement : Verify that DUT responds with status code FabricConflict if an AddNOC is attempted using a different NodeID but the same Root CA Certificate and Fabric ID. [Fabric uniqueness enforcement]
Requirement : Verify that the returned status code is LabelConflict if two fabrics attempt to use the same label. [Fabric label collision handling]
Requirement : Verify that fabrics list contains the expected number of entries and that each entry accurately reflects the correct Label and VendorID. [FabricDescriptorStruct, list integrity]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends ArmFailSafeResponse Command to TH1 with field ErrorCode as 'OK'(0) when TH1 sends ArmFailSafe command with ExpiryLengthSeconds set to 0. [Failsafe disengagement]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds to an AddNOC command with a status code FabricConflict when a NOC and ICAC with conflicting fabric information are provided. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends ArmFailSafeResponse Command to TH1 with field ErrorCode as 'OK'(0) when ArmFailSafe command is sent with ExpiryLengthSeconds set to 0. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the TrustedRootCertificates list read from the DUT contains trusted_root_original_size + 2 entries. [OPCREDS.S]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends ArmFailSafeResponse Command to TH1 with field ErrorCode as 'OK'(0) when ArmFailSafe command is sent with the ExpiryLengthSeconds field set to failsafe_max. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the AddTrustedRootCertificate Command succeeds by sending the status code as SUCCESS when RootCACertificate is added. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status code CONSTRAINT_ERROR to an AddNOC command when a constraint violation occurs (NOC, ICAC relationship). [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends NOCResponse Command with StatusCode of InvalidFabricIndex when an invalid fabric index is used in RemoveFabric command. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the number of fabrics in the Fabrics List is fabrics_original_size + 2 after fabric add operations. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends NOCResponse Command with StatusCode of OK when RemoveFabric command is issued with correct FabricIndex. [OPCREDS.S]

Requirement : Verify that the CurrentFabricIndex attribute matches the accessing fabric index in the DUT and references an entry in the fabric list, when read by a controller on its fabric. [OPCREDS.S]

Requirement : Verify that a fabric-filtered read of the Fabrics attribute returns an entry with FabricIndex, RootPublicKey, VendorID, and FabricID matching the accessing fabric credentials. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS to a RemoveFabric command with a valid FabricIndex. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with FAILSAFE_REQUIRED when the UpdateNOC command is sent without failsafe being armed. [OPCREDS.S]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT returns a CSRResponse when CSRRequest is sent with IsForUpdateNOC set to false. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with CONSTRAINT_ERROR when UpdateNOC command is sent with a NOC and ICAC chain not matching CSRRequest purpose. [OPCREDS.S]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with a NOCResponse with the StatusCode field set to InvalidPublicKey if the UpdateNOC certificate public key does not match the CSRRequest. [OPCREDS.S]

Reference: Section 28.1.4, TC-SC-1.4 MRP message counter and duplicate messaging - PROVISIONAL
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds to duplicate messages (same message counter as previous) with an acknowledgement but takes no further action. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Reference: Section 28.2.1, TC-SC-2.1 Session Establishment - Passcode Authenticated Session Establishment (PASE) - PROVISIONAL
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that during PASE session establishment, the DUT, acting as responder, receives a TLV-encoded PBKDFParamRequest message with the protocol header and fields correctly constructed as per specification. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Reference: Section 28.2.1, TC-SC-2.1 Session Establishment - Passcode Authenticated Session Establishment (PASE) - PROVISIONAL
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that during PASE session establishment, the Initiator receives a TLV-encoded PBKDFParamResponse message from the DUT, with correct protocol header and field contents as per specification. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify that the Initiator receives the PBKDFParamResponse message and that the protocol header is properly constructed with: Message Flags: S Flag is set to 0, and DSIZ field is set to 0; Session ID is set to 0; Security Flags: Session Type bits are set to 0; Exchange Flags: I Flag is set to 0; Protocol Opcode is set to 33 (0x21); Protocol ID is set to 0.

Requirement : If possible in debug mode, verify that the PBKDFParamResponse message contains: 1. initiatorRandom from the PBKDFParamRequest message, 2. responderRandom (randomly generated 32-bit octet string), 3. responderSessionId (max size 16-bits unsigned integer; no overlap with existing responder session IDs), 4. pbkdf_parameters inclusion based on hasPBKDFParameters flag from the request, and 5. responderSessionParams (optional session-parameter-struct: SESSION_IDLE_INTERVAL, SESSION_ACTIVE_INTERVAL, SESSION_ACTIVE_THRESHOLD [32-bit unsigned]).

Requirement : Verify that the Responder receives the Pake1 message and the protocol header is properly constructed with: Message Flags: S Flag is set to 0, DSIZ field is set to 0; Session ID is 0; Security Flags: Session Type bits are 0; Exchange Flags: I Flag is 1; Protocol Opcode is 34 (0x22); Protocol ID is 0.
Requirement : If possible in debug mode, verify that the Pake1 message contains pA as a 65-bit octet string.

Requirement : Verify that the Initiator receives the Pake2 message with proper protocol header: Message Flags: S Flag is 0, DSIZ is 0; Session ID is 0; Security Flags: Session Type bits are 0; Exchange Flags: I Flag is 0; Protocol Opcode is 35 (0x23); Protocol ID is 0.
Requirement : If possible in debug mode, verify that the Pake2 message contains pB (65-bit octet string) and cB (32-bit octet string).

Requirement : Verify that the Responder receives the Pake3 message and the protocol header is properly constructed: Message Flags: S Flag is 0, DSIZ is 0; Session ID is 0; Security Flags: Session Type bits are 0; Exchange Flags: I Flag is 1; Protocol Opcode is 36 (0x24); Protocol ID is 0.
Requirement : If possible in debug mode, verify that the Pake3 message contains cA (32-bit octet string).

Requirement : Verify that the Initiator receives the status report/PakeFinished message containing: GeneralCode set to SUCCESS (0), ProtocolId to SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and ProtocolCode to SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS (0x0000). Confirm the initiator has not sent encrypted data prior to receiving PakeFinished.

Requirement : When the DUT acts as responder and receives a PBKDFParamRequest with an invalid passcodeID, verify DUT sends a status report to the initiator with a FAILURE general code (1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002). Confirm no further processing is performed after this report.

Requirement : When the DUT acts as responder and receives Pake3 with an invalid key confirmation message (cA), verify DUT sends a status report to the initiator with a FAILURE general code (1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002). Confirm no further processing is performed after this report.

Requirement : When the DUT acts as initiator and receives Pake2 with an invalid key confirmation message (cB), verify DUT sends a status report to the responder with a FAILURE general code (1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002). Confirm no further processing is performed after this report.
Requirement : After commissioning and session initiation, verify that the responder sends a response to a Read Request (e.g., reading OnOff attribute from OnOff Cluster).

Requirement : Verify that the initiator constructs and sends a TLV-encoded Sigma1 message containing a random initiation value, destination identifier, session identifier, the public key for its ephemeral key pair, and optional MRP parameters, with message header: I Flag set to 1, S flag and DSIZ fields set to 0, Session Type field set to 0, Protocol ID field set to 0, Protocol Opcode field set to 48 (0x30).
Requirement : Verify that the initiator receives the Sigma2 message from the responder after Sigma1 is sent.

Requirement : Verify that the Sigma2 message has: I flag set to 0, S flag and DSIZ fields set to 0, message payload with sigma-2-struct TLV encoding, Session Type set to 0, Protocol ID set to 0, and Protocol Opcode set to 49 (0x31).

Requirement : Verify that the Sigma 2 message has the I flag set to 0 (Responder), S flag and DSIZ fields set to 0 (Source/Destination Node IDs not present), message payload follows sigma-2-struct TLV encoding, Session Type is 0 (Unicast), Protocol ID is 0 (PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL), and Protocol Opcode is 49 (0x31) [CASE Sigma2].

Requirement : Verify that the session is established and the responder sends a SigmaFinished StatusReport message with GeneralCode SUCCESS, ProtocolId PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, and ProtocolCode SessionEstablishmentSuccess.

Requirement : Verify that the initiator constructs and sends a TLV-encoded Sigma3 message with encrypted integrity data, I flag set to 1 (Initiator), S flag and DSIZ fields set to 0, payload follows sigma-3-struct TLV encoding, Session Type is 0, Protocol ID is 0, and Opcode is 50 (0x32) [CASE Sigma3].
Requirement : Verify that the responder sends response to Read Request from Initiator.

Requirement : Verify that the Initiator sends and the Responder receives a Sigma1 message with resumption containing initiatorRandom, initiatorSessionId, destinationId, resumptionID, initiatorResumeMIC, initiatorEphPubKey, initiatorSessionParams.

Requirement : Verify Sigma2_Resume message is properly formatted: I Flag is 0, S flag/DSIZ 0, Session Key Type 0, Protocol ID 0x0000, Opcode 0x33; resumptionID is Octet String (≤16 bytes), responderSessionID is uint16, sigma2ResumeMIC is Octet String (≤16 bytes).
Requirement : Verify that the Initiator sends a SigmaFinished message to the Responder to complete the session resumption.
Requirement : Verify that the responder sends a Read Response matching the previously saved 'read response' value after session resumption.

Requirement : Verify that if Sigma1 message received with resumptionID but no initiatorResumeMIC, DUT sends a status report with FAILURE, Protocol ID SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), Protocol Code INVALID_PARAMETER (0x0002), and does not proceed further.

Requirement : Verify that if Sigma1 message received with initiatorResumeMIC but no resumptionID, DUT sends a status report with FAILURE, Protocol ID SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), Protocol Code INVALID_PARAMETER (0x0002), and does not proceed further.

Requirement : Verify DUT falls back to normal CASE if Sigma1 carries a resumptionID with an invalid initiatorResumeMIC and sends a status report with SUCCESS, Protocol ID SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS (0x0000).

Requirement : Verify that a Sigma1 with an invalid destinationId causes DUT to send a status report with FAILURE, Protocol ID SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), Protocol Code NO_SHARED_TRUST_ROOTS (0x0001), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that sending a Sigma3 message with improperly generated encrypted integrity data causes DUT to send a status report with FAILURE, Protocol ID SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), Protocol Code INVALID_PARAMETER (0x0002), and does not proceed further.
Requirement : Verify that the Session Key Type field in Sigma1 and Sigma2_Resume messages is set to 0 during session resumption procedures.

Requirement : Verify maximum lengths and types for session resumption parameters: initiatorRandom (≤32 bytes Octet String), initiatorSessionId (uint16), destinationId (Octet String), resumptionID (≤16 bytes Octet String), initiatorEphPubKey (Octet String), initiatorResumeMIC (Octet String), responderSessionParams (uint32 for SESSION_IDLE_INTERVAL, SESSION_ACTIVE_INTERVAL, SESSION_ACTIVE_THRESHOLD).
Requirement : Verify that the Protocol ID is set to 0x0000 and Protocol Opcode set to expected values (Sigma1: 0x30, Sigma2_Resume: 0x33) in session resumption message formatting.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma1 message with an initiatorResumeMIC and no resumptionID, it sends a status report to the TH with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma1 message with a resumptionID and an invalid initiatorResumeMIC, it falls back to establishing CASE without resumption and sends a status report with SUCCESS general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS (0x0000).

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma1 message with an invalid destinationId, it sends a status report to the TH with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of NO_SHARED_TRUST_ROOTS (0X0001), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with improperly generated encrypted integrity data (TBEData3Encrypted) after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report to the TH with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with invalid initiatorNOC data after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with invalid initiatorICAC data after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with invalid signature data after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with invalid initiatorEphPubKey data after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT receives a Sigma3 message with invalid responderEphPubKey data after a valid Sigma1 and Sigma2 exchange, it sends a status report with a FAILURE general code, Protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002), and performs no further processing.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an initiator, sends a status report with a FAILURE general code (value 1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002) and performs no further processing after receiving a corrupt TBEData2Encrypted in Sigma2 during CASE Handshake.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an initiator, sends a status report with a FAILURE general code (value 1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002) and performs no further processing after receiving a corrupt responderNOC in Sigma2 during CASE Handshake.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an initiator, sends a status report with a FAILURE general code (value 1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002) and performs no further processing after receiving a corrupt responderICAC in Sigma2 during CASE Handshake.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an initiator, sends a status report with a FAILURE general code (value 1), protocol ID of SECURE_CHANNEL (0x0000), and Protocol Code of INVALID_PARAMETER (0X0002) and performs no further processing after receiving a corrupt Signature in Sigma2 during CASE Handshake.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT is able to handle at least three CASE sessions on a per-fabric basis, by supporting at least 15 active subscriptions from five different fabrics and providing the correct attribute value updates to each subscriber.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT is commissioned and put in Commissioning Mode using Open Basic Commissioning Window command, it starts advertising Commissionable Node Discovery service using DNS-SD.

Requirement : Verify that the DUT advertises Commissionable Node Discovery DNS-SD subtypes according to requirements, including instance name as a 64-bit random hex string, service type _matterc._udp, service domain .local, and appropriate subtypes (e.g., _L<dddd> for discriminator, _CM for commissioning mode, etc.), depending on its connectivity and capability flags.

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT is rebooted, then put in Commissioning Mode using Open Basic Commissioning Window command, it starts advertising Commissionable Node Discovery service using DNS-SD.

Requirement : Verify that after rebooting and entering Commissioning Mode using the Open Basic Commissioning Window command, the DUT starts advertising Commissionable Node Discovery service using DNS-SD. [CADMIN.S.C01.Rsp]

Requirement : Check that the DUT advertises a DNS-SD instance name as a 64-bit randomly selected ID expressed as a sixteen-character hex string with capital letters, different from the one at a previous step. [MCORE.COM.WIFI, MCORE.COM.ETH, MCORE.COM.THR]
Requirement : Verify that the DNS-SD service type for the DUT advertisement is _matterc._udp. [MCORE.COM.WIFI, MCORE.COM.ETH, MCORE.COM.THR]

Requirement : Verify that the DNS-SD service domain for the DUT advertisement is .local, except for Unicast DNS on Thread networks, where it must be as configured by the Thread Border Router. [MCORE.COM.WIFI, MCORE.COM.ETH, MCORE.COM.THR]

Requirement : For WiFi or Ethernet, verify that the target hostname is derived from the 48bit or 64bit MAC address expressed as a twelve or sixteen capital letter hex string, using a randomized version if privacy randomization is enabled. [MCORE.COM.WIFI, MCORE.COM.ETH]

Requirement : For Thread, verify that the target hostname is derived from the 48bit or 64bit MAC extended address as a twelve or sixteen capital letter hex string, using a randomized version if privacy randomization is enabled. [MCORE.COM.THR]

Requirement : Verify that the DNS-SD subtype _L<dddd> is present, representing the 12-bit long discriminator as a variable-length decimal number in ASCII text without leading zeros. [MCORE.SC.VENDOR_SUBTYPE, MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE]

Requirement : Verify that the DNS-SD subtype _S<dd> is present, representing the 4-bit short discriminator as a variable-length decimal number in ASCII text without leading zeros. [MCORE.SC.VENDOR_SUBTYPE, MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE]

Requirement : If the VENDOR_SUBTYPE is present, verify that the subtype _V<ddddd> is present and is a 16-bit vendor id in variable-length decimal number ASCII text without leading zeros. [MCORE.SC.VENDOR_SUBTYPE]

Requirement : If the DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE is present, verify that subtype _T<ddd> is present; the value must match the device type from Data Model and be in variable-length decimal number ASCII without leading zeros. [MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE]
Requirement : Verify that subtype _CM is present in the DNS-SD advertisement for Commissioning Mode. [MCORE.SC.VENDOR_SUBTYPE, MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE]
Requirement : Verify that after the Open Basic Commissioning Window timeout expires, the DUT stops sending DNS-SD advertisements. [CADMIN.S.C01.Rsp]

Requirement : When the DUT is instructed to enter commissioning mode using the Open Commissioning Window command, verify that it resumes sending Commissionable Node Discovery DNS-SD advertisements. [CADMIN.S.C00.Rsp]

Requirement : Verify again all DNS-SD subtypes and structure required for the advertisements after the Open Commissioning Window command is sent, including instance name, service type, service domain, target hostname, and all valid subtypes. [MCORE.COM.WIFI, MCORE.COM.ETH, MCORE.COM.THR, MCORE.SC.VENDOR_SUBTYPE, MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE]
Requirement : Verify that after the Open Commissioning Window timeout expires, the DUT stops sending DNS-SD advertisements. [CADMIN.S.C00.Rsp]
Requirement : If Extended Discovery is enabled, verify that the DUT starts to send Commissionable Node Discovery DNS-SD advertisements. [MCORE.SC.EXTENDED_DISCOVERY]
Requirement : If Extended Discovery is enabled, verify that all DNS-SD requirements are still respected but subtype _CM must NOT be present. [MCORE.SC.EXTENDED_DISCOVERY]

Requirement : Verify that the hostname in the returned record is a fixed-length twelve-character (or sixteen-character) hexadecimal string, encoded as ASCII (UTF-8) text using capital letters. [Hostname]
Requirement : If supports_lit is false, verify that the ICD key is NOT present in the TXT record. [ICD TXT key]
Requirement : If supports_lit is true, verify that the ICD key IS present in the TXT record, and it has the value of 0 or 1 (ASCII). [ICD TXT key]
Requirement : If supports_icd is true and supports_lit is false, set sit_mode to true. [SII TXT key]
Requirement : If supports_icd is true and supports_lit is true, set sit_mode to true if ICD=0, otherwise set sit_mode to false. [SII TXT key]
Requirement : If supports_icd is false, set sit_mode to false. [SII TXT key]
Requirement : If sit_mode is true, verify that the SII key IS present in the TXT record. [SII TXT key]
Requirement : If the SII key is present, verify it is a decimal value with no leading zeros and is less than or equal to 3600000 (1h in ms). [SII TXT key]
Requirement : If supports_icd is true, verify that the SAI key is present in the TXT record. [SAI TXT key]
Requirement : If the SAI key is present, verify it is a decimal value with no leading zeros and is less than or equal to 3600000 (1h in ms). [SAI TXT key]
Requirement : If the SAT key is present, verify it is a decimal value with no leading zeros and is less than or equal to 65535. [SAT TXT key]
Requirement : If the SAT key is present and supports_icd is true, verify that the value is equal to active_mode_threshold. [SAT TXT key]
Requirement : Verify that the AAAA record contains at least one IPv6 address. [AAAA]

Requirement : In a DNS-SD browse for _I<hhhh>._sub._matter._tcp.local, verify that the returned DNS-SD instance name is a fixed-length sixteen-character hexadecimal string, encoded as ASCII (UTF-8) text using capital letters. [DNS-SD Instance Name]
Requirement : Verify that the PTR record returned from a DNS-SD browse for _matter._tcp.local has the DNS-SD instance name set as expected. [PTR Record DNS-SD Instance Name]

Requirement : If MCORE.SC.TCP is true, verify that the T TXT key value is 4 (Bit index 2, 0x100 — indicating TCP Server support) or 6 (0x110 indicating both TCP Client and TCP Server support). [T TXT key]
Requirement : If MCORE.SC.TCP is false, verify that the T TXT key value is 0 (indicating only MRP is supported) or the field is not present in the TXT record. [T TXT key]

Requirement : In Commissioner Discovery as Commissioner, verify that the AAAA records published by the DUT for each IPv6 address use a DNS-SD instance name that is a 64-bit randomly selected ID, expressed as a sixteen-character hexadecimal string with capital letters. [DNS-SD Instance Name]

Requirement : For Commissioner Discovery as Commissioner, verify that the service type in the AAAA record is _matterd._udp and the service domain is .local (or as configured by Thread Border Router for Thread networks). [Service Type and Domain]

Requirement : In Commissioner Discovery as Commissioner, verify that the target hostname is derived from the 48-bit MAC address, expressed as a twelve-character capital letter hexadecimal string (randomized version each time if MAC is randomized). [Target Hostname]
Requirement : If (MCORE.SC.DEVTYPE_SUBTYPE) is present, verify that the _T<ddd> subtype is present and <ddd> is a variable length decimal number in ASCII without leading zeros. [Device Type Subtype]
Requirement : If (MCORE.SC.VP_KEY) is present, verify that VP key contains at least Vendor ID, and if Product ID is present, values are separated by a + sign. [VP KEY]
Requirement : If (MCORE.SC.DT_KEY) is present, verify that DT key contains the device type identifier encoded as a variable length decimal ASCII number without leading zeros. [DT KEY]
Requirement : If (MCORE.SC.DN_KEY) is present, verify that DN key is UTF-8 encoded. [DN KEY]
Requirement : In Commissioner Discovery as Commissionee, verify that the DUT can discover DNS-SD commissioner advertisements from the TH. [DNS-SD Discovery]
Requirement : Verify that nodes commissioned on the same fabric have the same compressed fabric ID. [Compressed Fabric ID]
Requirement : Verify that a node removed from fabric and commissioned back to the same fabric has the same compressed fabric ID. [Compressed Fabric ID]

Reference: Section 28.5.1, Test TCP Connection Establishment with DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device, when acting as a TCP client on a Matter network, is able to initiate and establish a CASE session with a remote TCP server device, requesting a session that supports large payloads. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.2, Test CASE Session allowing large payloads set up over TCP Connection with DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that, after initiating a CASE session with the server device, the client device is able to establish a session where large payloads are supported over the underlying TCP connection. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.3, Test CASE Session becomes inactive after underlying TCP Connection with DUT is dropped
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device marks the CASE session as inactive and unusable if the underlying TCP connection with the server device is closed. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.4, Test Back to back TCP Connection establishment, disconnection and re-establishment with DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device can re-establish a new CASE session over TCP with the server device after the previous session had been closed and marked inactive. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.5, Test InvokeCommandRequest and CommandResponse over a TCP-based CASE session established with DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device, after establishing a TCP-based CASE session with the server device, can successfully initiate and receive a response to an InvokeCommandRequest over this session. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.6, Test a Large Payload interaction over a TCP-based CASE session with DUT via a wildcard Read operation
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device, over a TCP-based CASE session with the server device, is able to read all attributes of all clusters and receive all attribute data in a single ReportData message that may exceed the 1280 byte IPv6 MTU for MRP. [MCORE.SC.TCP]

Reference: Section 28.5.7, Test that an IM operation(possible over MRP) can use an already existing TCP-based session with DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the client device, when sending a regularly-sized payload, is able to use an already existing TCP-based session to initiate an IM operation, particularly if a preferred MRP session does not exist. [MCORE.SC.TCP]
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) has access privileges for the GroupKeyMap attribute implemented on the server [GRPKEY.C.A0000].
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) has access privileges for the GroupKeyTable attribute implemented on the server [GRPKEY.C.A0001].
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) implements sending the KeySetWrite command [GRPKEY.C.C00.Tx].
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) implements sending the KeySetRead command [GRPKEY.C.C01.Tx].
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) implements sending the KeySetRemove command [GRPKEY.C.C03.Tx].
Requirement : Verify that the client device (DUT) implements sending the KeySetReadAllIndices command [GRPKEY.C.C04.Tx].
Requirement : Verify that the endpoint supported for Groups cluster on the client device (DUT) is as provided by the DUT Manufacturer [PIXIT.G.ENDPOINT].
Requirement : The client device SHALL correctly read the MaxGroupKeysPerFabric attribute from the GroupKeyManagement cluster on the DUT and store its value for future operations. [GRPKEY.S.A0003]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command with three valid EpochKeys and incremental EpochStartTimes and verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS (0x00). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : Upon sending a KeySetRead command for a written GroupKeySetID, the client device SHALL verify that the returned EpochKey fields are null, EpochStartTime fields match those sent in the previous KeySetWrite, and GroupKeySecurityPolicy is TrustFirst (0). [GRPKEY.S.C01.Rsp(KeySetRead), GRPKEY.S.C02.Tx(KeySetReadResponse)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where only EpochKey0 and EpochStartTime0 are non-null, and verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS (0x00). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where EpochKey0 and EpochKey1 and their corresponding EpochStartTime fields are non-null and EpochKey2 is null, and verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS (0x00). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where EpochKey0 is null and at least one later EpochKey is non-null, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where EpochStartTime0 is null and at least EpochKey0 is non-null, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where EpochStartTime0 is set to 0, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command with EpochKey1 as null but EpochStartTime1 as non-null, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command with EpochKey1 as non-null and EpochStartTime1 as null, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]

Requirement : The client device SHALL send a KeySetWrite command where EpochStartTime1 is less than EpochStartTime0, and verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND (0x85). [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp(KeySetWrite)]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND(0x85) when a KeySetWrite command is sent with EpochStartTime1 earlier than EpochStartTime0. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND(0x85) when EpochKey1 and EpochStartTime1 are null and EpochKey2 and EpochStartTime2 are not null in a KeySetWrite command. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND(0x85) when EpochKey2 is null and EpochStartTime2 is not null in a KeySetWrite command. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND(0x85) when EpochKey2 is not null and EpochStartTime2 is null in a KeySetWrite command. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status INVALID_COMMAND(0x85) when EpochStartTime2 is earlier than EpochStartTime1 in a KeySetWrite command. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status CONSTRAINT_ERROR(0x87) when EpochKey1 and/or EpochKey2 are sent with a value less than 16 bytes or greater than 16 bytes in a KeySetWrite command. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS(0x00) when KeySetWrite command is sent with valid and ascending EpochStartTime values for all EpochKeys. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]

Requirement : Verify that the EpochStartTime values match the values sent in the previous KeySetWrite when read back using KeySetRead, and that all EpochKeys are returned as null in the response. [GRPKEY.S.C01.Rsp, KeySetRead]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status NOT_FOUND(0x8b) when a KeySetRead command is sent with a GroupKeySetID that does not exist. [GRPKEY.S.C01.Rsp, KeySetRead]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with status SUCCESS(0x00) when a KeySetRemove command is sent for an existing GroupKeySetID. [GRPKEY.S.C03.Rsp, KeySetRemove]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT allows writing KeySetWrite commands until the Max_GrpKey entries are reached in the GroupKeyMap attribute list for the given fabric. [GRPKEY.S.C00.Rsp, KeySetWrite]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT (Commissionee) responds with a correct AttestationResponse when it receives an AttestationRequest command with a valid AttestationNonce from the commissioner. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT does not accept an invalid AttestationNonce (not 32 bytes) in the AttestationRequest command, and responds with an INVALID_COMMAND error. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the AttestationElements field size in AttestationResponse does not exceed 900 bytes. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the certification_declaration in AttestationResponse is present, has a valid format, and fulfills the structure and field constraints described. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify that the attestation_nonce present in AttestationElements matches the AttestationNonce that was sent in the AttestationRequest and is exactly 32 bytes long. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify cryptographically that the AttestationSignature from AttestationResponse is valid by verifying it against the concatenated AttestationElements and attestation challenge, using the public key in the device's DAC. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that CertificateChainRequest for both DAC and PAI certificate types returns an X.509v3 certificate not exceeding 600 bytes. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Validate that the DUT, acting as Commissioner [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER], properly handles an invalid AttestationResponse received from the TH during the commissioning process and responds with the correct warning message indicating the device is not genuine or attestation failed via its error handling APIs or user interface.
Requirement : Ensure that the commissioning operations are conducted within the Fail-Safe Context [11.9.7.1].

Requirement : Verify that the DUT can distinguish the error condition where the Commissionee device is not yet certified, by detecting an invalid AttestationResponse and providing a corresponding warning or error state.

Requirement : For each certificate set specified in the 'Certificates for TC-DA-1.4' table, verify that commissioning fails with a clear indication that the device is not genuine or an attestation failure, as observed in the DUT's error handling APIs or user interface.
Requirement : Verify that after a failed attestation scenario, the TH is factory reset and can become commissionable again.
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT, acting as Commissionee [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE], generates NOCSR Information using the CSRResponse Command during the NOCSR procedure.
Requirement : Check that the NOCSR Information generated by the DUT includes a signature created with the Device Attestation Private Key.
Requirement : Verify that the Certificate Signing Request (CSR) generated by the DUT follows the encoding and rules from PKCS #10.
Requirement : Validate that the Node Operational Key Pair generated by the DUT is unique.
Requirement : Ensure that the DUT, acting as Commissionee, rejects invalid CSRNonce values sent by the test harness (TH1) during the NOCSR procedure.
Requirement : Verify that the Device Under Test (DUT) generates the NOCSR Information using the CSRResponse Command. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT-generated NOCSR Information includes a signature using the Device Attestation Private Key. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the Certificate Signing Request (CSR) follows the encoding and rules specified by PKCS #10. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT generates a unique Node Operational Key Pair for each request. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT rejects invalid CSRNonce values sent by the test harness (TH1). [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the length of the nocsr_elements_message is less than or equal to 900 bytes. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the CSR is a DER-encoded octet string. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the CSR specifies the Public Key algorithm id-ecPublicKey with curve prime256v1 (ASN1 OID) / P-256 (NIST). [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the CSR specifies a 256-bit public key. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the CSR signature algorithm is ecdsa-with-SHA256 and that the signature is valid. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the returned CSRNonce field is an octet string of length 32 and matches the CSRNonce sent in the request. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that nocsr_attestation_signature signs nocsr_tbs using the public key in dac. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : If present, verify that attestation_challenge does not appear in any of the vendor_reserved fields within the nocsr_elements_message. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify that commissioning DUT2 within the same device family as DUT1 (same VID/PID) completes successfully, and the device attestation certificates are properly signed. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that, for DUT1, a CertificateChainResponse containing a valid PAICertificate is returned upon request. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that, for DUT1, a CertificateChainResponse containing a valid DACCertificate is returned upon request. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the Authority Key Identifier in the PAI certificate is signed by a PAA in the DCL and is not one of the blacklisted PAAs specified. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the issuer field of dac_cert is byte-for-byte equivalent to the subject field of pai_cert. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that the Authority Key Identifier extracted from the PAI certificate is signed by a PAA in the DCL and is not one of the SDK’s test PAAs [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].

Requirement : Verify that the PAI authority key ID must not be one of the following: 78:5C:E7:05:B8:6B:8F:4E:6F:C7:93:AA:60:CB:43:EA:69:68:82:D5 or 6A:FD:22:77:1F:51:1F:EC:BF:16:41:97:67:10:DC:DC:31:A1:71:7E [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].
Requirement : Verify that the DAC certificate’s issuer field is byte-for-byte equivalent to the PAI certificate’s subject field [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].
Requirement : Verify that the PAI certificate’s issuer field is byte-for-byte equivalent to the PAA certificate’s subject field [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].
Requirement : Verify that the extracted public key (pk_1) from the first DAC does not match the public key (pk_2) from the second DAC [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].
Requirement : Verify that the TH is successfully commissioned for each certificate set described in the 'Certificates for TC-DA-1.8' table [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].

Requirement : For each Revocation Test Case, verify that the DUT provides a clear indication to the user that the device may not be genuine if either the DAC or PAI certificate is revoked [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].
Requirement : For each Revocation Test Case, verify that the DUT proceeds with commissioning without warning when both DAC and PAI certificates are valid and not revoked [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONER].

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, when acting as a Client, sends the Invoke Request Message to the Test Harness (TH), and the message contains one valid CommandDataIB referencing a specific Endpoint, Cluster, and Command. [MCORE.IDM.C.InvokeRequest]

Requirement : Verify that the TH device receives a request message from the DUT with the same command paths as those provided in the single Invoke Request when sending one valid CommandDataIB [MCORE.IDM.C.InvokeRequest.BatchCommands].
