Requirement : Verify that the client sends a subscription request where MinIntervalFloor is greater than MaxIntervalCeiling, and expect the server to reply with an error and no subscription is established. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : Verify that the client can subscribe to a specific global attribute from all clusters on all endpoints by sending a subscription request with the appropriate AttributePath, and that the subscription succeeds with attribute values for the global attribute returned. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : Verify that the client can subscribe to a global attribute on a specific endpoint across all clusters, and that only data for that endpoint is sent back. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : Verify that the client sends a subscription request with both AttributeRequests and EventRequests set to empty, and expects that the subscription does not succeed, with the server sending a Status Response Action having INVALID_ACTION Status Code. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : Verify that the Device Under Test (DUT) sends a Timed Request Message to the Test Harness (TH) with a timeout value in milliseconds, and then sends an Invoke Request Message to the TH after receiving the status response from the TH, ensuring the Timed Request Message is unicast and the Invoke Request is sent before the specified timeout, with the TimedRequest field set to TRUE. [MCORE.IDM.C.InvokeRequest]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a Timed Request Message to the TH with a timeout value in milliseconds, and then sends a Write Request Message to the TH after receiving the status response, ensuring the Timed Request is unicast, the Write Request is sent within the timeout, and the TimedRequest field in the Write Request is set to TRUE. [MCORE.IDM.C.WriteRequest]

Requirement : Verify that when the TH does not send a response to the DUT for a Timed Request, the DUT does not send a follow-up message to the TH. [MCORE.IDM.C.WriteRequest OR MCORE.IDM.C.InvokeRequest]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a status response when it receives a Timed Request Action from the TH. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends back a Write Response after performing the write action when it receives a Timed Write Transaction request, and that the write action can be confirmed by sending a ReadRequest. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : For devices of Matter release 1.4 or later, verify that the DUT responds with status TIMEOUT (0x94) if the Invoke Request Message is sent after the timeout. For Matter 1.3 or earlier, verify that the DUT responds with TIMEOUT (0x94) or UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e). [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an event reader client, sends a Read Request Message to the TH for a supported event, including the fields EventRequests (valid EventPathIB), optional EventFilters, and optional FabricFiltered (bool). [MCORE.IDM.C.ReadRequest]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, acting as an event subscriber client, sends a Subscribe Request Message to the TH for a supported event, including the fields KeepSubscriptions (bool), MinIntervalFloor (uint16), MaxIntervalCeiling (uint16), EventRequests (valid EventPathIB), optional EventFilters, and optional FabricFiltered (bool). [MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest]

Requirement : Verify that the Subscribe Request Message sent by the DUT to the Test Harness (TH) for a supported event includes the following fields: KeepSubscriptions (bool), MinIntervalFloor (uint16), MaxIntervalCeiling (uint16), EventRequests (list of valid EventPathIB), and optionally EventFilters (list of minimum event numbers per node) and FabricFiltered (bool). EventPathIB must be from the Valid Event Paths table and not target a group. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify that when the TH sends a Report Data message in response to the DUT's Subscribe Request Message, the DUT sends a Status Response Action with a success Status Code. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify that if the TH does not respond with a Report Data message after the DUT's Subscribe Request Message, the Subscribe interaction is terminated and the DUT can send another Subscribe Request Message for events. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify that if the DUT sends a Subscribe Request Message, and after the TH sends a Report Data message and the DUT sends a Status Response Message, the TH does not respond with a Subscribe Response message, the Subscribe interaction is terminated and the DUT can send another Subscribe Request Message for events. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify that with an active Event subscription, when the TH sends a Report Data message to the DUT with an invalid SubscriptionId, the DUT sends a Status Response action with an INVALID_SUBSCRIPTION Status Code. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify that with an active Event subscription, when the TH sends a Report Data message to the DUT after the maximum interval from the last Report Data, the DUT sends a Status Response action with an INVALID_SUBSCRIPTION Status Code. [MCORE.IDM.C, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeRequest, MCORE.IDM.C.SubscribeEvent]

Requirement : Verify for every writable attribute in all clusters on all endpoints that if a Write Request with an out of bounds value is made, the DUT sends a Status Response Action with a CONSTRAINT_ERR or Status Code. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : Verify that for every cluster on every endpoint, all mandatory global attributes (ClusterRevision, FeatureMap, AttributeList, AcceptedCommandList, GeneratedCommandList) are present.

Requirement : Verify that for every cluster on every endpoint, AttributeList, AcceptedCommandList, and GeneratedCommandList do not contain duplicate values and every value in the list is in the required range.

Requirement : For every cluster on every endpoint, verify that each attribute in the AttributeList matches exactly with the set of attributes returned by a wildcard read for that cluster and endpoint.
Requirement : For every standard cluster on every endpoint, ensure none of the global attributes contain any additional values outside the ranges defined in the specification.
Requirement : Verify that global attributes for every cluster on every endpoint do not contain values with prefixes outside the allowed ranges for standard or test_vendor MEI prefixes.
Requirement : Verify that none of the global attributes for every cluster on every endpoint have MEI values outside the allowed suffix ranges.
Requirement : Verify that each cluster ID for every cluster on every endpoint has a prefix in the standard or MEI allowed range.
Requirement : For every cluster on every endpoint, verify that if the cluster ID is in the standard prefix range, it is a known cluster ID as defined in the specification.
Requirement : For every cluster on every endpoint, verify that if the cluster ID is in the MEI prefix range, its suffix is within the manufacturer suffix range (0xFC00 - 0xFFFE).
Requirement : For every standard cluster on every endpoint, verify that FeatureMap only contains known feature flags for that cluster.
Requirement : After performing a wildcard read of all events, verify that at least one event is returned.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, confirm that the cluster is not marked as provisional in the specification.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, ensure all features in the FeatureMap are known features as per the specification.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, for each feature listed in the FeatureMap, validate that the feature is allowed according to the assessed specification conformance.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, verify that all attributes in the standard range are known attributes as defined in the specification.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, ensure every attribute in the AttributeList is allowed according to the conformance assessment of the specification.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, verify that all commands listed in the AcceptedCommands list within the standard range are known from the specification.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, ensure all commands listed in the GeneratedCommands list are valid as per the specification and are allowed for the cluster.
Requirement : For every server cluster on every endpoint, ensure the ClusterRevision is the highest revision listed in the specification.

Requirement : For every standard cluster present on an endpoint, ensure the server-side PICS code for the cluster is present in the PICS file if the cluster is present on the endpoint, and not present if the cluster is absent.

Requirement : For every attribute in every standard cluster on an endpoint, if the attribute ID is in the AttributeList, ensure the server-side PICS code for the attribute is present in the PICS file and not present otherwise.
Requirement : For every cluster, ensure PICS codes for all accepted commands exactly match the commands listed in the AcceptedCommands list of the implementation.
Requirement : For every cluster, ensure PICS codes for all generated commands exactly match the commands listed in the GeneratedCommands list of the implementation.
Requirement : For every feature in every cluster, ensure the PICS file matches the features marked in the FeatureMap for the endpoint.
Requirement : Ensure the PICS_SDK_CI_ONLY PICS code is not set.
Requirement : If a root node device type is present on the endpoint, verify the MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE PICS code is set.
Requirement : If the device supports onboarding payload (QR or NFC), verify the manual pairing code PICS (MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC) is set.

Requirement : For every cluster present in the spec, for every feature in the cluster: If the cluster is present on the endpoint and the feature is marked in the feature map, ensure the PICS code for the feature is present in the PICS file. Otherwise, ensure the feature PICS code is not present in the PICS file. [PICS]
Requirement : Check that the PICS_SDK_CI_ONLY PICS is not set. [PICS]
Requirement : If the device has a root node device type on this endpoint, ensure the MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE PICS code is set. [PICS]
Requirement : If the device has any onboarding payload (MCORE.DD.QR or MCORE.DD.NFC), it has the manual pairing code PICS set (MCORE.DD.MANUAL_PC). [PICS]
Requirement : If any of the above PICS checks failed, fail the test. [PICS]

Requirement : For each endpoint, ensure that the device type is present in the spec, all mandatory server cluster IDs are present in the Descriptor cluster ServerList, and no disallowed clusters are present. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If PIXIT.allow_extra_clusters is not set, ensure all the clusters present in the ServerList are in allowed_clusters. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For each standard device type in the Descriptor DeviceTypeList, ensure the revision is the highest device type revision listed in the spec. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For every returned attribute that is of type string and not null or empty, ensure the value is a valid UTF-8-encoded string. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the device supports Managed Device feature (MNGD) of the ACL Cluster, ensure that instructions are available to remove all access restrictions from DUT before testing. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : Create a MatterTlvJson dump of the wildcard attributes for submission to certification. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_test_in_product_name flag is not set, check that 'Test' does not appear in the product name. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_test_in_vendor_name flag is not set, check that 'Test' does not appear in the vendor name. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_default_vendor_id flag is not set, check that the product does not use a test vendor ID. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : If the pixit_allow_default_calendar_format flag is not set, and if the TimeFormatLocalization cluster with the ActiveCalendarType attribute is present, check that the calendar format is not the default. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_unit_testing_cluster flag is not set, ensure that the unit testing cluster does not appear on any endpoint. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_fault_injection_cluster flag is not set, ensure that the fault injection cluster does not appear on any endpoint. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_sample_mei_cluster flag is not set, ensure that the sample mei cluster does not appear on any endpoint. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If the pixit_allow_empty_fixed_label_list flag is not set, and the FixedLabel cluster is present, ensure that the fixed label cluster list is not empty. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : If the pixit_allow_fixed_label_default_values flag is not set, and the FixedLabel cluster is present, ensure that the fixed label cluster list does not contain any of the default labels specified in the spec. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : If any accidental defaults check fails, fail the test. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : For each root-node-restricted cluster in the list (ACL, Time Synchronization, TLS Certificate Management, TLS Client Management), ensure the cluster does not appear on any endpoint that is not the root node. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For endpoints with Window Covering device type revision 5 or lower, ensure the ServerList does not contain Closure Dimension or Closure Control clusters. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For endpoints with Closure device type with revision 1, ensure the ServerList does not contain the Window Covering cluster. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For endpoints with Closure Panel device type with revision 1, ensure the ServerList does not contain the Window Covering cluster. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For endpoints with Closure device type (any revision), ensure the ServerList does not contain the Closure Dimension cluster. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : For endpoints with Closure Panel device type (any revision), ensure the ServerList does not contain the Closure Control cluster. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : For endpoints with Closure device type, ensure the TagList in the Descriptor contains exactly one tag from Closure namespace (0x44), no tag from Closure Panel namespace (0x45), and any other permissible tag. [MCORE.IDM.S]

Requirement : For endpoints with Closure Panel device type, ensure the TagList in the Descriptor contains exactly one tag from Closure Panel namespace (0x45), no tag from Closure namespace (0x44), and any other permissible tag. [MCORE.IDM.S]
Requirement : Verify that the client device implements the Admin Commissioning Cluster as a client [CADMIN.C].

Requirement : Verify that the client device can send the OpenBasicCommissioningWindow command to support the Basic Commissioning Method (BCM) [CADMIN.C.C01.Tx(OpenBasicCommissioningWindow)].
Requirement : Verify that the client device can send the RevokeCommissioning command to revoke a commissioning window [CADMIN.C.C02.Tx(RevokeCommissioning)].
Requirement : Verify that the client device can send the RemoveFabric command to remove a fabric from the server [OPCREDS.C.C0a.Tx(RemoveFabric)].
Requirement : Verify that the client device has access privileges for the Fabrics attribute implemented on the server [OPCREDS.C.A0001(Fabrics)].
Requirement : Verify that the client device has access privileges for the NodeLabel attribute implemented on the server [BINFO.C.A0005(NodeLabel)].
Requirement : Verify that the client device has access privileges for the WindowStatus attribute implemented on the server [CADMIN.C.A0000(WindowStatus)].

Requirement : Verify that the Node to be commissioned hosts an 'Administrator Commissioning Cluster' as described in Section 11.18 [CADMIN.S].
Requirement : Verify that the 'Administrator Commissioning Cluster' exposes a command to enter commissioning mode, callable over a secure channel [CADMIN.S].
Requirement : Verify that, during the commissioning window, the Node maintains existing configuration including network and identity, and allows normal interactions from other nodes [CADMIN.S].
Requirement : Verify that when the Commissioning Timeout parameter of the OpenCommissioningWindow (OCW) command is set, it is within the correct range (>=3m and ⇐15m) [CADMIN.S].

Requirement : Verify the Node successfully responds to write and read commands issued to its Basic Information Cluster's NodeLabel mandatory attribute [BINFO.C.A0005].

Requirement : When acting as BDX Sender and Initiator, verify that the SendInit message sent by the DUT contains the following mandatory fields: at least one Proposed Transfer Control bit (RECEIVER_DRIVE or SENDER_DRIVE), Range Control, Proposed Max Block Size, File Designator Length, and File Designator. Start Offset, Proposed Max Length, and Metadata are optional. [MCORE.BDX.Sender, MCORE.BDX.Initiator]

Requirement : When acting as BDX Receiver and Initiator, verify that the ReceiveInit message sent by the DUT contains the following mandatory fields: at least one Proposed Transfer Control bit (RECEIVER_DRIVE or SENDER_DRIVE), Range Control, Proposed Max Block Size, File Designator Length, and File Designator. Start Offset, Proposed Max Length, and Metadata are optional. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver, MCORE.BDX.Initiator]

Requirement : Verify that the ReceiveInit message sent by the DUT has at least one of the PTC[RECEIVER_DRIVE] or PTC[SENDER_DRIVE] bits set in the Proposed Transfer Control field, includes Range Control, Proposed Max Block Size, File Designator Length, and File Designator. Start Offset and Proposed Max Length are optional. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver]

Requirement : Verify that the SendAccept message sent by the DUT has exactly one transfer control mode chosen from the proposed ones by the Initiator, has the newest version supported by the Responder not newer than Initiator's proposed version, Max Block Size <= proposed Max Block Size, and optionally Metadata. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder]

Requirement : Verify that the ReceiveAccept message sent by the DUT has exactly one transfer control mode chosen from the proposed by Initiator, version as the newest supported by Responder not newer than proposed, includes Range Control, Max Block Size <= proposed Max Block Size, and, if Length is present, matches the rules for definite length. Metadata is optional. [MCORE.BDX.Sender] [MCORE.BDX.Responder]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error FILE_DESIGNATOR_UNKNOWN when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message with an unsupported file designator. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error START_OFFSET_NOT_SUPPORTED when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message with an invalid or unsupported start offset. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error LENGTH_TOO_LARGE when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message with a definite length field too large for the DUT. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error LENGTH_TOO_SHORT when receiving a SendInit message with a definite length field too short for the DUT. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error LENGTH_REQUIRED when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message with a missing definite length field. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error TRANSFER_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message with a proposed transfer control method not supported by the DUT. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a StatusReport message with Error RESPONDER_BUSY when receiving a SendInit or ReceiveInit message when the DUT is too busy to process another transfer. [MCORE.BDX.Receiver] [MCORE.BDX.Responder] [MCORE.BDX.Sender]

Requirement : Verify that, in synchronous file sending, the Block message sent by DUT as BDX Sender has Block Counter starting at 0, Data length in the range (0 < Length <= Max Block Size), and further Blocks are sent in ascending and sequential block counter order. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousSender]

Requirement : Verify that the BlockEOF message sent by DUT as BDX Sender in synchronous transfer includes Block Counter and Data field within the range (0 <= Length <= Max Block Size), and that the pre-negotiated file size was transferred if a definite size had been given. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousSender]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT, as BDX Receiver, sends BlockAck messages with Block Counter corresponding to the Block being acknowledged, and that BlockQuery messages (if MCORE.BDX.Driver) start at block counter 0 and are sent in ascending order. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver] [MCORE.BDX.Driver]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT, as BDX Receiver, can send a BlockQueryWithSkip message with Block Counter and BytesToSkip fields. [MCORE.BDX.BlockQueryWithSkip]
Requirement : Verify that the BlockAckEOF message sent by DUT as BDX Receiver must include Block Counter matching the BlockEOF being acknowledged. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver]

Requirement : Verify that the ReceiveInit message sent by the DUT for restarting a synchronous file receiving session has at least one of PTC[RECEIVER_DRIVE] or PTC[SENDER_DRIVE] bits set, Range Control, Proposed Max Block Size, Start Offset set to the first byte desired, File Designator Length, and File Designator. Proposed Max Length and Metadata are optional. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 1, Section 49.2.3, Synchronous File Receiving
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a ReceiveInit message to the Test Harness (TH) with the following fields: Proposed Transfer Control - at least one of the PTC[RECEIVER_DRIVE] or PTC[SENDER_DRIVE] field bits is set, Range Control, Proposed Max Block Size, Start Offset (set to the offset of the first byte desired in the first block transferred), Proposed Max Length (optional), File Designator Length, File Designator, and Metadata (optional). [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 2, Section 49.2.3, Synchronous File Receiving
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a BlockAck message to the Test Harness (TH) with the Block Counter field matching the Block Counter embedded in the Block being acknowledged. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 3, Section 49.2.3, Synchronous File Receiving
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a BlockAckEOF message to the Test Harness (TH) with the Block Counter field matching the Block Counter embedded in the BlockEOF being acknowledged. [MCORE.BDX.SynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 1, Section 49.2.4, Asynchronous File Sending
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a SendInit message to TH, followed by a Block message with Block Counter set to 0 at the start of the transfer, and the Data length in the range [0 < Length ⇐ Max Block Size] as negotiated in the SendAccept message. [MCORE.BDX.AsynchronousSender]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 2, Section 49.2.4, Asynchronous File Sending
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends blocks in ascending and sequential block counter order in asynchronous file sending mode. [MCORE.BDX.AsynchronousSender]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 3, Section 49.2.4, Asynchronous File Sending
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the BlockEOF message sent by the DUT in asynchronous file sending mode contains the Block Counter, and Data length in the valid range, and that the pre-negotiated file size is transferred if specified. [MCORE.BDX.AsynchronousSender]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 1, Section 49.2.5, Asynchronous File Receiving
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a ReceiveInit message to TH in asynchronous receiving mode with the Proposed Transfer Control having the PTC[ASYNC] field bit set. [MCORE.BDX.AsynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 2, Section 49.2.5, Asynchronous File Receiving
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a BlockAckEOF message to the TH in asynchronous receiving mode with the Block Counter corresponding to the BlockEOF being acknowledged. [MCORE.BDX.AsynchronousReceiver]

Reference: Test Procedure, Step 1, Section 52.1.1, OTA Provider Discovery
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT is able to invoke the AnnounceOTAProvider command on the OTA Requestor (OTA-R), including the ProviderNodeID of the OTA Provider (OTA-P), Vendor Id of the invoking node, AnnouncementReason set to SimpleAnnouncement, optional MetadataForNode, and Endpoint number of the OTA-P. [MCORE.ACL.Administrator]

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT sends a QueryImage command to the OTA Provider, the VendorID, ProductID, and SoftwareVersion fields match the values reported by the Basic Information Cluster attributes of the DUT. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the ProtocolsSupported field in the QueryImage command includes BDX Synchronous protocol, and HTTPS if the device supports MCORE.OTA.HTTPS. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor, MCORE.OTA.HTTPS]
Requirement : Verify that if HardwareVersion is present in the QueryImage command, it matches the HardwareVersion attribute of the DUT's Basic Information Cluster. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : If the Location field is present in the QueryImage command, verify it matches the DUT's Basic Information Cluster Location Attribute. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : If MCORE.OTA.RequestorConsent is set, verify that the RequestorCanConsent field in the QueryImage command is True; otherwise it should be False. [MCORE.OTA.RequestorConsent]
Requirement : Verify with the TH/OTA-P that the QueryImage command from the DUT is sent only once within a 2 minute interval. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : Verify that after an AnnounceOTAProvider command, the DUT queries the OTA Provider at the indicated ProviderLocation on its next scheduled OTA Provider query. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : When receiving a QueryImageResponse with QueryStatus set to 'UpdateAvailable' and a valid ImageURI, verify the DUT transfers the software image from the TH/OTA-P. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : When QueryImageResponse has QueryStatus set to 'Busy' with DelayedActionTime, verify that the DUT does not send a QueryImage before at least 2 minutes (120 seconds) have passed since the previous QueryImage command. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If QueryImageResponse has QueryStatus set to 'Busy' and DelayedActionTime set to 3 minutes, verify the DUT waits at least 3 minutes before issuing another QueryImage, and that the transfer occurs upon the next 'UpdateAvailable' response. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : When QueryImageResponse has QueryStatus set to 'UpdateAvailable' with ImageURI using HTTPS, verify that the DUT downloads the image from the specified HTTPS URL. [MCORE.OTA.HTTPS]

Requirement : If QueryImageResponse contains a Software Version that is the same or lower than the DUT's current version, verify that the DUT does not start a software image transfer. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : If QueryImageResponse contains an invalid BDX ImageURI, verify that the DUT does not start a software image transfer. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If RequestorCanConsent is True, and the OTA Provider sets UserConsentNeeded to True, verify that the DUT obtains user consent before transferring the software update image. [MCORE.OTA.RequestorConsent]

Requirement : When starting an image transfer after 'UpdateAvailable,' verify that the DUT's maximum requested block size is no more than 1024 bytes over non-TCP, and no more than 8192 bytes over TCP. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : When the ImageURI is an HTTPS URL, verify that the DUT downloads the software image from the HTTPS URL rather than from the OTA provider. [MCORE.OTA.HTTPS]

Requirement : If the image transfer is interrupted and fails, verify that the DUT waits for an Idle timeout of no less than 5 minutes before another transfer attempt, and that a new transfer is started upon re-issuing a QueryImage command. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If the image transfer is interrupted and resumed, verify that the DUT sets the RC[STARTOFS] bit and STARTOFS field to indicate resumption of a previously aborted transfer, and only receives the missing part of the software image. [MCORE.OTA.Resume]

Requirement : After the image is transferred and before applying the update, verify that an ApplyUpdateRequest sent by the DUT includes the correct UpdateToken and NewVersion fields (matching those from QueryImageResponse and the downloaded image version). [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If OTA Provider responds to ApplyUpdateRequest with Action 'Proceed' and DelayedActionTime 0, verify that the DUT immediately applies the update, and that SoftwareVersion attribute is updated and no further ApplyUpdateRequest is sent. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If ApplyUpdateResponse sets Action 'Proceed' with DelayedActionTime nonzero (e.g., 3 minutes), verify that the DUT waits the specified delay before applying the update and updating the SoftwareVersion attribute. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If ApplyUpdateResponse sets Action 'AwaitNextAction', verify DUT waits at least the minimum interval (2 minutes) before sending ApplyUpdateRequest again and does not apply the update in this period. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If the second ApplyUpdateResponse to a repeated ApplyUpdateRequest sets Action 'Proceed', verify that the DUT waits the indicated (e.g., 3 minute) delay before applying the update, updating SoftwareVersion accordingly. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : If ApplyUpdateResponse sets Action 'Discontinue', verify that the DUT clears the previously downloaded image, resets UpdateState attribute to Idle, does not send NotifyUpdateApplied, and retains the original SoftwareVersion. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Reference: Section 11.19.6.1, 52.4.3. [TC-SU-2.6] NotifyUpdateApplied Command from DUT to OTA-P
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT clears its previously downloaded software image and resets the UpdateState Attribute to Idle after receiving an ApplyUpdateResponse command with Action field set to 'Discontinue'. [OTAR.C.M.ApplyUpdateResponse]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT does not send the NotifyUpdateApplied command within a reasonable time after receiving an ApplyUpdateResponse with Action set to 'Discontinue'. [OTAR.C.M.NotifyUpdateApplied]
Requirement : Verify the SoftwareVersion attribute from the Basic Information cluster of the DUT is the same as it was previously after receiving 'Discontinue' action. [DGGEN.S.A0002]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends the NotifyUpdateApplied Command to the OTA-P after installing the software update, and that the UpdateToken matches the one provided in the QueryImageResponse and the SoftwareVersion matches the version downloaded. [OTAR.C.M.NotifyUpdateApplied]
Requirement : Verify that the BootReason attribute in the General Diagnostics cluster returned by the DUT is SoftwareUpdateCompleted after update application. [DGGEN.S.A0004(BootReason)]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for all the state changes (Querying, Downloading, Applying, Idle) when a software update is performed on the DUT. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for the state change to DelayedOnQuery when the DUT receives a QueryImageResponse with QueryStatus set to 'Busy'. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for the state change to Idle when the DUT sends a QueryImage command and receives no response. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for the state change to DelayedOnUserConsent when RequestorCanConsent is set to True in QueryImage and UserConsentNeeded is True in QueryImageResponse. [MCORE.OTA.RequestorConsent]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for the state change to Idle, a DownloadError event notification (with SoftwareVersion, BytesDownloaded, ProgressPercent, PlatformCode data) when an error occurs during the OTA image download and BDX Idle timeout occurs. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the OTA-Subscriber receives a StateTransition event notification for the state change to DelayedOnApply when ApplyUpdateResponse with Action='AwaitNextAction' is sent. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the VersionApplied event is generated by the DUT whenever a new version starts executing after a software update, and that the SoftwareVersion and ProductID match the attributes in the Basic Information Cluster for the new version. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a QueryImage command to the correct OTA Provider on the commissioned fabric, and that the mandatory fields (VendorId, ProductId, HardwareVersion, SoftwareVersion, ProtocolsSupported, RequestorCanConsent, Location) are populated correctly. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that if the QueryImage command to the first OTA Provider fails after a finite number of attempts, the DUT sends a QueryImage command to the secondary OTA Provider on another fabric. [MCORE.OTA.Retry]
Requirement : Verify that, after a successful QueryImageResponse from the secondary OTA Provider, the software image transfer occurs between the provider and the DUT. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a QueryImageResponse Command to the OTA-R/TH when it receives a QueryImage Command, and that the Status field is set to NotAvailable. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a QueryImageResponse Command to the OTA-R/TH with the ProtocolSupported field listing BDX, and that the image URI field has the scheme 'bdx' (lowercase), the authority field contains only the Operational Node ID in uppercase hexadecimal (16 characters), and that the URI starts with 'bdx://', is at least 24 characters long, contains a valid path, and does not include user, query, or fragment fields. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]
Requirement : Verify that when ProtocolSupported is listed as 'https', the URI present in QueryImageResponse is a valid https string. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT responds to a QueryImage command with QueryStatus set to 'Busy' and DelayedActionTime to 3 minutes, a subsequent QueryImage command results in a QueryImageResponse with UpdateAvailable status, and the OTA-R/TH starts downloading the correct update image from the DUT. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that when OTA-R/TH sends a QueryImage Command (with BDX supported), OTA-R/TH starts the download from the DUT, and that the DUT uses Receiver-Drive mode for non-TCP and Asynchronous mode for TCP transport. The actual block size for transfers is the negotiated maximum block size (except last block), with maximum block sizes following protocol constraints (power-of-two if >128 bytes, exact if 16-128 bytes, >=1024 for non-TCP, >=4096 for TCP). The image downloaded must match the intended image. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that if the RequestorCanConsent field is True in a QueryImage command from TH, and DUT responds with UserConsentNeeded set to True, the DUT does not try to obtain user consent prior to transfer. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that if the RequestorCanConsent field is True in a QueryImage command from TH, and DUT responds with UserConsentNeeded set to False, the DUT tries to obtain user consent from the user prior to transfer. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that if an image transfer is forced to fail before completion, the DUT can restart the image transfer from the beginning after a new QueryImage request, and that the BDX Idle timeout is at least 5 minutes. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that if an image transfer is aborted and a Resume (RC[STARTOFS]) is requested, the DUT resumes the previously aborted transfer and completes sending the image to the TH. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that upon receiving an ApplyUpdateRequest Command after image download, the DUT sends an ApplyUpdateResponse Command with non-empty Action field set to 'Proceed' and a DelayedActionTime that is zero or nonzero. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT responds to an ApplyUpdateRequest with Action set to 'AwaitNextAction' and DelayedActionTime to 3 minutes, on the subsequent ApplyUpdateRequest the DUT sets Action to 'Proceed' and DelayedActionTime per the spec. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]

Requirement : Verify that when the DUT's ApplyUpdateResponse Command Action field is set to 'Discontinue' after completion of image download and ApplyUpdateRequest from OTA-R/TH, the entire OTA process is restarted on DUT when OTA-R/TH sends another QueryImage Request. [MCORE.OTA.Provider]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT allows writing the DefaultOTAProviders Attribute for each fabric, and no errors are returned. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT successfully updates the DefaultOTAProviders Attribute to the correct device per fabric when read after successful write. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that when multiple provider locations are written for the DefaultOTAProviders Attribute on the same fabric, the write operation fails with CONSTRAINT_ERROR status code 0x87, and the attribute value is unchanged or per spec. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT accepts a write with an empty provider list for the DefaultOTAProviders Attribute without error, and that subsequent reads reflect the update accordingly. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : Verify that the UpdatePossible attribute on the DUT is set to True when a software update is possible. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that the UpdateState Attribute on the DUT is set to one of the allowed values: Unknown, Idle, Querying, DelayedOnQuery, Downloading, Applying, DelayedOnApply, RollingBack, DelayedOnUserConsent. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]
Requirement : Verify that during software image download by the DUT, the UpdateStateProgress attribute provides a percent value representing download progress. [MCORE.OTA.Requestor]

Requirement : Verify that using a vendor specific mechanism for OTA Update (if OTA Requestor cluster is not supported), the DUT starts updating its software, and that after the update, the SoftwareVersion attribute reflects the updated version. [MCORE.OTA.VendorSpecific]

Reference: 52.11.1, [TC-SU-5.1] Verifying vendor specific OTA implementation on DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT is able to successfully perform a software update by vendor-specific means if the OTA Requestor cluster is not supported. [MCORE.OTA.VendorSpecific]

Reference: 52.11.1, [TC-SU-5.1] Verifying vendor specific OTA implementation on DUT
Requirement: Requirement : Verify that the DUT starts updating its software when the OTA Update is performed using the vendor specific mechanism, and confirm that the SoftwareVersion attribute matches the version downloaded for the software update. [MCORE.OTA.VendorSpecific]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced for reading attributes on the DUT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced for writing attributes on the DUT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced for invoking commands on the DUT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced for reading events on the DUT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced when existing privileges are removed from subjects on the DUT. [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]

Requirement : Verify that when a TH writes the NodeLabel attribute in the Basic Information cluster with a string requiring manage privilege, the result is UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e) if the privilege is not granted. [UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS, NodeLabel, Basic Information]

Requirement : Verify that when a TH sends the TestEventTrigger command to the General Diagnostics cluster with parameters requiring manage privilege, the result is UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e) if the privilege is not granted. [UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS, TestEventTrigger, General Diagnostics]

Requirement : Confirm that after ACL attribute is written granting only administer privilege on the Access Control cluster and view privilege elsewhere, a TH does not gain administer privileges outside of the Access Control cluster. [Administer privilege, ACL, Access Control cluster]

Requirement : Confirm that after ACL attribute is written granting only administer privilege on the Access Control cluster and view privilege elsewhere, a TH does not gain manage privileges on any cluster. [Manage privilege, ACL, Access Control cluster]

Requirement : Confirm that after ACL attribute is written granting only administer privilege on the Access Control cluster and view privilege elsewhere, a TH retains view privileges elsewhere. [View privilege, ACL]

Requirement : Verify that a TH writing ACL attribute with a single entry granting only Administer privilege on the Access Control cluster results in success. [Administer privilege, ACL, Access Control cluster]

Requirement : Verify that a TH writing ACL attribute with a single entry granting only Administer privilege on the Access Control cluster does not grant administer privileges on other clusters. [Administer privilege, ACL, Access Control cluster]

Requirement : Verify that a TH writing ACL attribute with a single entry granting only Administer privilege on the Access Control cluster does not grant manage privileges on any cluster. [Manage privilege, ACL, Access Control cluster]

Requirement : Verify that restoring full access to a node by updating the ACL attribute with a null target and Administer privilege is successful. [ACL, Administer privilege, full access]

Requirement: Requirement : Verify that access control is enforced for new subscriptions: TH2 can only subscribe to view-privilege attributes/events after its privileges are changed via ACL updates. [Access Control, Subscriptions, Privilege enforcement]

Requirement : Verify that after ACL changes, TH2 cannot subscribe to administer-privilege attributes or events (should return INVALID_ACTION). [Access Control, Administer privilege, Subscription, INVALID_ACTION]

Requirement : Verify that when the ACL entry for a subject is reduced from Manage or Operate to View privilege, they continue to receive view-privilege attribute reports but lose access to higher-privilege actions. [Access Control, Subscription, Privilege changes]
Requirement : Verify that when TH1 is granted 'view' privilege on Endpoint 0, it can successfully read the Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute on Endpoint 0. [N1, view privilege]
Requirement : Verify that when TH2 is not granted explicit access, reading the Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute on Endpoint 0 results in UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e). [N2, access denied]

Requirement : Verify that when TH1 and TH2 are granted 'view' privilege on Endpoint 0, both can successfully read the Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute, while TH3 is denied. [N1, N2, view privilege]
Requirement : Verify that when TH1 and TH3 are granted 'view' privilege for Endpoint 0, only TH1 and TH3 have access while TH2 is denied. [N1, N3, view privilege]
Requirement : Verify that when TH2 and TH3 are granted 'view' privilege on Endpoint 0, only TH2 and TH3 have access while TH1 is denied. [N2, N3, view privilege]

Requirement : Verify that when all three (TH1, TH2, TH3) are granted 'view' privilege on Endpoint 0, all can successfully read the Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute. [N1, N2, N3, view privilege]
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced when subjects field includes multiple node IDs (i.e., granting multiple nodes the same privilege).
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced when the AccessControlEntryStruct targets a specific cluster (e.g., AccessControl cluster) on a specific endpoint.
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced when the AccessControlEntryStruct targets a specific endpoint without specifying a cluster.
Requirement : Verify that access control is correctly enforced when the AccessControlEntryStruct targets a subject group, such as a list of node IDs or a certification authority subject.

Requirement : Verify that wildcard targets (null Subjects and/or Targets in AccessControlEntryStruct with privilege View) allow any node to access attributes unless more restrictive ACEs exist.

Requirement : Verify that when the ACL attribute is configured with View privilege on AccessControl cluster at Endpoint 0 and Descriptor cluster at Endpoint PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT, reading the DeviceTypeList attribute from the Descriptor cluster on Endpoint PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT returns SUCCESS. Requirement : Verify that an ACL entry with View privilege targeting only PIXIT.ACE.APPCLUSTER at Endpoint PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT allows reading the corresponding attribute (PIXIT.ACE.APPATTRIBUTE) from that cluster and endpoint, and returns SUCCESS. Requirement : Verify that providing View access in the ACL for the Descriptor cluster (0x001d), specific to Endpoint PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT, allows reading DeviceTypeList from that endpoint but restricts the same attribute at Endpoint 0 to UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e). [ACL, Descriptor, DeviceTypeList, PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT]

Requirement : Verify that granting View access in the ACL for the DeviceType RootNode (0x0016) enables reading DeviceTypeList at Endpoint 0 but restricts access to PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT. [ACL, Descriptor, DeviceTypeList, RootNode]

Requirement : Verify that granting View access in the ACL for the DeviceType PIXIT.ACE.APPDEVTYPEID allows reading DeviceTypeList at PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT but not at Endpoint 0. [ACL, Descriptor, DeviceTypeList, PIXIT.ACE.APPDEVTYPEID]

Requirement : The device under test (DUT) SHALL allow read access to its Endpoint PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute only when the requester has appropriate ACL permissions. [PIXIT.ACE.APPENDPOINT]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL return UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS (0x7e) when a read request is made for an attribute for which the requester does not have access privileges as per its ACL. [UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL allow writing of ACL entries to its AccessControl cluster when performed by an admin with Administer privilege. [AccessControl cluster, Administer]

Requirement : The DUT SHALL restrict reads to the Descriptor cluster DeviceTypeList attribute on endpoint 0 unless a View privilege is granted for that cluster/endpoint. [Descriptor cluster, DeviceTypeList]

Requirement : When wildcard reads are performed, the DUT SHALL respond only for endpoints and clusters for which the requester is authorized and MUST NOT report any status (even UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS) for unauthorized endpoints. [Wildcard Read]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL enforce access control with respect to the fabric of the request, using only ACLs from the same fabric as the requester. [Multi-fabric, FabricIndex]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL differentiate ACL authorization for multiple fabrics and only enforce permissions defined for the requester's current fabric. [FabricIndex]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL provide access to basic attributes only if the requester has appropriate View privilege in the ACL for the target fabric. [Basic Information cluster, View]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL remove fabric-specific ACL entries upon receiving a RemoveFabric command with the corresponding FabricIndex. [RemoveFabric, FabricIndex]
Requirement : The DUT SHALL enforce access control for group messaging using only ACLs with group auth mode. [Group auth mode]

Requirement : When a KeySetWrite command is sent in the GroupKeyManagement cluster using a key pre-installed on the Test Harness, the DUT SHALL respond with SUCCESS if the command is valid. [GroupKeyManagement, KeySetWrite]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a SUCCESS response when the KeySetWrite command is sent in the GroupKeyManagement cluster with a key pre-installed on the TH and valid GroupKeySet fields. [DUT, GroupKeyManagement]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends a SUCCESS response when the GroupKeyMap attribute list is updated to bind several GroupIds with GroupKeySetIDs in the GroupKeyManagement cluster. [DUT, GroupKeyMap]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with SUCCESS when a valid AddGroup Command is sent to the Groups cluster on the specified endpoint over CASE. [DUT, Groups]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends SUCCESS response when the ACL attribute in the Access Control cluster is updated to add Manage privileges for a group and maintain administrative privileges for the TH. [DUT, Access Control]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS when AddGroup is attempted by a subject without appropriate access privileges. [DUT, Groups]

Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends SUCCESS response when revoking group Management access and restoring full access to the TH via ACL attribute update in the Access Control cluster. [DUT, Access Control]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with SUCCESS to a ViewGroup Command on the Groups cluster to confirm that AddGroup commands were processed as expected. [DUT, Groups]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with NOT_FOUND to a ViewGroup Command for groups where AddGroup was not expected to succeed. [DUT, Groups]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT responds with SUCCESS to RemoveAllGroups Command on the Groups cluster. [DUT, Groups]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends SUCCESS response when the GroupKeyMap attribute list is reset to an empty list in the GroupKeyManagement cluster. [DUT, GroupKeyMap]
Requirement : Verify that the DUT sends SUCCESS response when the KeySetRemove command is sent for specific GroupKeySetIDs in the GroupKeyManagement cluster. [DUT, GroupKeyManagement]

Requirement : Verify that all attributes on the DUT require the appropriate access control privilege for reading, such that reading returns attribute value only if acl_privilege is met, otherwise UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS is returned. [DUT, Access Control, Attribute Read]

Requirement : Verify that all attributes on the DUT require the appropriate access control privilege for writing, non-writable attributes cannot be written, and responses comply with privilege and writability as specified (SUCCESS, UNSUPPORTED_WRITE, UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS as per context). [DUT, Access Control, Attribute Write]

Requirement : Validate that the DUT implements the WebRTC Transport Provider cluster if the variable WEBRTCP.S is set to true.
Requirement : Validate that the DUT implements the WebRTC Transport Requester cluster if the variable WEBRTCR.C is set to true.

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT (Camera) SHALL respond to a ProvideOffer command containing an SDP Offer and null WebRTCSessionID with a ProvideOfferResponse that includes an allocated WebRTCSessionID. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL include the same WebRTCSessionID, originally allocated during session initiation, in all subsequent Answer and ICECandidates commands for the session. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL send a non-empty SDP string in the Answer command after receiving a ProvideOffer command initiating a session. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL respond with a SUCCESS status code upon receiving the ProvideICECandidates command. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL send ICECandidates command with a non-empty ICE candidate list after session establishment. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.1, Validate that setting an SDP Offer successfully initiates a new WebRTC session
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL respond to an EndSession command for an active session ID with a SUCCESS status code. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.2, Validate that providing an existing WebRTC session ID with an SDP Offer successfully triggers the re-offer flow
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL respond to a ProvideOffer command referencing an existing active WebRTCSessionID by triggering the re-offer flow, updating the session's SDP details, and returning the same session ID. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.2, Validate that providing an existing WebRTC session ID with an SDP Offer successfully triggers the re-offer flow
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL wait up to 30 seconds to send an Answer command with a non-empty SDP string in response to a re-offer flow trigger. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.3, Validate Deferred Offer Flow for Battery-Powered Camera in Standby Mode
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL support the SolicitOffer flow for battery-powered cameras, setting the DeferredOffer parameter to TRUE if additional initialization time (up to 30 seconds) is required to provide an Offer. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.4, Validate Non-Deferred Offer Flow for Battery-Powered Camera in Standby Mode
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL provide a SolicitOfferResponse with DeferredOffer set to FALSE and immediately send an Offer command with non-empty SDP, when no delay is necessary. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.5, Validate that the camera (DUT) can start a WebRTC session by issuing an ProvideOffer command - PROVISIONAL
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL be capable of initiating a WebRTC session by sending a ProvideOffer command with a non-empty offer SDP to the controller, and handle the standard session setup and teardown flow. [WEBRTCR.S][WEBRTCP.C]

Reference: Section 72.1.6, Validate Two-Way-Talk Full-Duplex support in camera(DUT)
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL support Full-Duplex Two-Way-Talk audio exchange, confirmed by returning FullDuplex TwoWayTalkSupportTypeEnum value for the TwoWayTalkSupport Attribute in AVSM. [WEBRTCP.S][WEBRTCR.C][AVSM.S]

Reference: Section 72.1.7, Validate that setting an SDP Offer sequentially from multiple camera controllers successfully initiates multiple WebRTC sessions
Requirement: Requirement : The DUT SHALL be able to handle sequential SDP Offers from multiple camera controllers, establishing multiple distinct WebRTC sessions, each with a unique session ID. [WEBRTCR.C][WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond to the ProvideOffer command with an allocated WebRTCSessionID when the WebRTCSessionID is null in the request. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The Answer command sent by the DUT to the controller must contain the same WebRTCSessionID as provided in the ProvideOfferResponse and contain a non-empty SDP string. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall successfully respond to the EndSession command with a SUCCESS status code when provided with the correct WebRTCSessionID. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall allow successful commissioning to multiple controllers on different fabrics. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall be capable of establishing a WebRTC session sequentially for multiple controllers issuing SDP Offers at different times. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall be capable of establishing multiple WebRTC sessions simultaneously for multiple controllers issuing SDP Offers before the first session is ended. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : Each WebRTC Answer command generated by the DUT must include a non-empty SDP string and the correct, corresponding WebRTCSessionID for each controller. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with a SUCCESS status code to each EndSession command when provided with the corresponding WebRTCSessionID by any controller. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall not have any active WebRTC sessions before initiating a new session with a controller during tests. [WEBRTCP.S]
Requirement : The number of PushAV Connections in the CurrentConnections attribute must be 0 before allocation of a new PushAV stream (or be cleared if otherwise). [PAVST.S]
Requirement : The SupportedFormats attribute from the PushAV Stream Transport Cluster on the DUT must not be empty and its values should be stored for further use. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : The AllocatedVideoStreams attribute from the CameraAVStreamManagement Cluster on the DUT must not be empty. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : The AllocatedAudioStreams attribute from the CameraAVStreamManagement Cluster on the DUT must not be empty. [PAVST.S]

Requirement : The DUT shall respond with an allocated ConnectionID, TransportOptions, and TransportStatus in the AllocatePushTransportResponse when the AllocatePushTransport command is sent with valid parameters and TriggerType set to Command. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code when the SetTransportStatus command is sent with the ConnectionID and TransportStatus set to Active. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : If the DUT supports the HardPrivacyModeOn attribute, enabling HardPrivacyModeOn must set the attribute to True. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : If HardPrivacyModeOn is enabled, the DUT shall respond to ManuallyTriggerTransport with INVALID_IN_STATE status code. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : Turning OFF HardPrivacyModeOn should set the attribute to False on the DUT. [PAVST.S]
Requirement : When SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled is set to True, the DUT shall respond to ManuallyTriggerTransport with INVALID_IN_STATE. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code when SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled is set to False. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS to ModifyPushTransport when StreamUsage is set to LiveView. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS when SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is set to True. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : When SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is True, the DUT shall respond to ManuallyTriggerTransport with INVALID_IN_STATE status code. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS when SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is set to False. [PAVST.S, AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : The DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status and begin transmission when ManuallyTriggerTransport is sent and all privacy requirements are satisfied. [PAVST.S]

Requirement : The controller must receive a PushTransportBegin Event from the DUT with connectionID, triggerType=Command, and activationReason=UserInitiated after the ManuallyTriggerTransport command initiates transmission. [PAVST.S]

Requirement : Verify that if the DUT supports HardPrivacyModeOn attribute, the HardPrivacyModeOn shall be turned OFF by following manufacturer provided instructions before testing SoftRecording and SoftLivestream privacy modes. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : When SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled is set to True, the DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : When SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled is set to False, the DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : When SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is set to True, the DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]
Requirement : When SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is set to False, the DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]

Requirement : If SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled or SoftLivestreamPrivacyModeEnabled is True, DUT shall reject ManuallyTriggerTransport command by responding with INVALID_IN_STATE status code. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]

Requirement : After all privacy modes are set to False, the DUT shall accept ManuallyTriggerTransport and begin transmission, responding with SUCCESS status code and a PushTransportBegin event that contains the correct connectionID, triggerType, and activationReason values. [AVSM.S.F03(PRIV)]

Requirement : The video playback must play while the DUT is uploading CMAF, and all extended paths must conform to the Matter-defined format: session_<SessionNumber>/<Track Name>/segment_<SegmentNumber>.<SegmentExtension> (with allowed extensions: m4s, init, mpd, m3u8).

Requirement : After deactivating a transport (SetTransportStatus = Inactive), CMAF content must still play successfully from uploaded files but DUT shall not upload any new files. All extended paths must still conform to the required format.
Requirement : When SetTransportStatus is set to Inactive, DUT shall respond with SUCCESS status code and generate a PushTransportEnd event with correct connectionID.
Requirement : Setting the TransportStatus to 'Active' must initiate transmission, and setting it to 'Inactive' must terminate transmission, when TriggerType is 'Continuous'.

Requirement : If privacy is enabled (either SoftRecordingPrivacyModeEnabled or SoftLiveStreamPrivacyModeEnabled), DUT must reject transmission commands with INVALID_IN_STATE until privacy is disabled.

Requirement : The device shall support a minimum of 5 fabrics for commissioning, each fabric with valid certificate chains as specified (at least one certificate per chain >= 400, 370, and 350 bytes respectively if not all certificates can be 400 bytes). [MCORE.ROLE.COMMISSIONEE]
Requirement : The device shall support writing the user-visible Label and NodeLabel attribute fields with a maximum size of 32 characters each and retain their values.

Requirement : The device shall support at least 4 Access Control entries per fabric, each having up to 4 subjects and 3 targets, supporting the minimum constraints for AccessControl EntriesPerFabric, SubjectsPerAccessControlEntry, and TargetsPerAccessControlEntry attributes.
